outcome_contract.why: xa-uploader currently hand-authors its CSS custom properties directly in globals.css with no structured source of truth or automated parity check. The brikette three-layer system provides a proven pattern for making tokens refactorable from TypeScript and preventing silent drift. Retrofitting xa-uploader closes the gap.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: xa-uploader tokens are declared in TypeScript source files under `packages/themes/xa-uploader/`, compiled into a committed CSS file, and a Jest parity test guards against future divergence between source and generated output.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: 17 `--gate-*` CSS vars in globals.css :root; 20 files consume them via @utility classes
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Risk of value mismatch between generated CSS and hand-authored expectation
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Parity test eliminates this risk; no visual change

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: No interactive state changes — pure token compilation
Theme tokens are static CSS values; no auth/PII surface
No runtime telemetry surface — build-time only
No existing theme parity test for xa-uploader; brikette has strong parity test template
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Gap: zero coverage on token correctness

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: `ThemeCSSConfig` interface must be satisfied; committed `.generated.css` is a new persisted artifact
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Risk: package exports map or tsconfig paths misconfigured
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Validate tsconfig path resolution works without changes (alias covers it)

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Generator script is a one-off CLI tool; no hot path
Generated CSS replaces inline :root block; must be equivalent
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Risk: missed token or value mismatch causes visual regression
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Parity test + visual diff during review covers this

stage_payload.scope_summary: xa-uploader currently declares ~17 CSS custom properties (`--gate-*`) directly inside its `globals.css` `:root` block. The brikette app has already been retrofitted with a three-layer generated theme system (`assets.ts` → `design-profile.ts` → `theme-css-config.ts` → `generateThemeCSS()` → committed `.generated.css` file + parity test). This fact-find investigates what is required to give xa-uploader the same structure, with a new `packages/themes/xa-uploader/` package, a generator script, and matching tests.
goals: Create `packages/themes/xa-uploader/` as a new `@themes/xa-uploader` workspace package.
Author a generator script that writes `apps/xa-uploader/src/app/theme-tokens.generated.css`.
Update `globals.css` to `@import` the generated file and remove the inline `:root` block.
Add a parity test (modelled on `packages/themes/brikette/__tests__/generated-parity.test.ts`) that validates the compiled output matches the committed `.generated.css`.
Keep the `[data-theme="dark"]` override block in `globals.css` as-is; it is application-level presentation logic, not brand tokens.
non_goals: Dark-mode token extraction: the `[data-theme="dark"]` block uses `--gate-*` overrides but xa-uploader is stated as light-mode only for the compiler layer. Dark overrides remain hand-authored in `globals.css`.
Changing any visual appearance of the xa-uploader app.
Changing the 20+ `@utility` classes or the `@keyframes pulse-slow` block in `globals.css`; those are application-layer CSS, not theme tokens.
constraints: xa-uploader uses `--webpack` (not Turbopack), consistent with how other non-turbopack apps in the monorepo are configured.
key_entry_points: `apps/xa-uploader/src/app/globals.css` — sole location of all `--gate-*` CSS custom properties; also contains `@utility` class definitions and `[data-theme="dark"]` overrides
`packages/themes/brikette/src/theme-css-config.ts` — reference implementation for the compiler mapping layer
`scripts/brikette/generate-theme-tokens.ts` — generator script pattern to replicate for xa-uploader

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Correctness, Advisory]: `colorVarMap` would produce wrong var names; requires `derivedVars.light` approach

rehearsal_issues.Scope Area: Generator script extras (color-scheme, profile vars, .dark block)
rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Correctness, Major]: compiler always emits `color-scheme` declarations and `--theme-transition-duration`; script must post-process to strip these
rehearsal_issues.Resolution Required: No — resolved: generator script post-processes output

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Correctness, Major]: `derivedVars.light` must reference brandColors values (not duplicate as string literals) to preserve single source of truth
rehearsal_issues.Resolution Required: No — resolved: config uses `Object.entries(assets.brandColors)` to populate derivedVars

rehearsal_issues.Issues Found: [Correctness, Advisory]: brikette has no "test" script; new package must add one explicitly
None — alias, deps, pnpm workspace all already cover new package
None — brikette parity test is a direct template
None — dark block is application-layer, correctly excluded
None — derivedVars approach requires zero component changes
[Correctness, Minor]: ThemeAssets requires fonts field; `fonts: {}` must be explicit in assets.ts

process_topology.note: None: local code path only. Token authoring is a file-level concern. There is no multi-step operator workflow, CI lane, approval path, or deployment orchestration change involved. The generator script will be invoked locally (like `pnpm --filter scripts brikette:generate-theme-tokens`) and the generated CSS is committed. No CI gate changes are required. ---
